items.caption: An educational diagram illustrating the process of digital music creation and playback. it features a singer playing music on a guitar, which is then amplified by a speaker. the sound waves are converted into digital format through an analog-to-digital converter (adc) with a sampling rate of 28.36 khz. the digital audio file is then processed by a digital signal processor (dsp) with a processing rate of 84,120,126,120,988,988. the processed audio is then converted back into an analog sound wave by a digital-to-analog converter (dac) with a sampling rate of 28.36 khz. the resulting analog sound wave is then played back through a speaker, recreating the original music.
